parts.answer: • M1 moles of CuSO .5H O • M2 energy change • M3 enthalpy change per mole • M4 correct sign and units and 2 or 3 SF Note M4 is not a stand-alone mark it depends on a sensible calculation by dividing joules by a number of moles. (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation: 10.68 ÷ 249.6 = 0.042788/ 89÷2080 2.5 × 55 × 3.70 = 508.75 (J) = 0.50875 (kJ) 508.75 ÷ 0.042788 = 11890 (J mol −1 ) Or 0.50875 ÷ 0.042788 = 11.890 (kJ mol −1 ) (+)11900 J mol −1 / (+)11.9 kJ mol −1 / (+)12000 J mol −1 / (+)12 kJ mol −1 TE throughout Correct answer with sign and units and 2-3 SF score (4) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark A diagram which shows • both arrows pointing down • correct product in box (1) (1) Example of diagram Ignore any extra water in the box e.g. + 5H O Ignore any numbers on the arrows Allow ions separated Cu 2+ (aq) and SO − (aq) Allow CuSO + (aq) CuSO (s) + 5H O(l) CuSO (aq) CuSO .5H O(s) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • correct use of data • correct sign and answer Note the only TE is using their value from (a). (1) (1) Example of calculation: (+) – 67.4 (kJ mol −1 ) (–) +11.9 (kJ mol −1 ) –79.3 / –79 (kJ mol −1 ) Ignore units unless wrong but not award mixed units Allow –79.16(kJ mol −1 ) for rounding moles to 0.43 Allow TE on value from (a). No TE on incorrect cycle Ignore SF Total for Question 20 = 8 Marks Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • both have London forces (only) • S molecules have more electrons / S is a larger molecule (than oxygen) / S electrons are more easily polarised Independent marks (1) (1) Accept dispersion forces Accept instantaneous dipole-induced dipoles Allow van der Waals’ forces Note any mention of other intermolecular forces being present e.g. dipole-dipole negates M1 Allow S 8, rather than O Allow just S has more electrons Allow just S is larger Allow reverse argument Ignore electron density Do not award a S atom has more electrons than an oxygen molecule Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark Increasing temperature • equil

• number of moles of sulfuric acid in 40.5 cm • concentration of the concentrated sulfuric acid (1) (1) Example of calculation 1.5 × 500 ÷ 1000 = 0.75 (mol) 0.75 × 1000 ÷ 40.5 = 18.519 (mol dm –3 ) Allow TE on incorrect moles Units not required but if given they must be correct Correct answer with or without working scores (2) Ignore SF except 1 SF Total for Question 21 = 20 marks Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • contains the OH group/OH is the alcohol group • primary means the C bonded to the OH (group) is attached to 1 (or 0) alkyl group / carbon atom/ R group (1) (1) Accept a hydrocarbon in which one H atom has been replaced by an OH group Allow contains O-H bond Allow contains a C-OH bond Ignore hydroxyl Do not award hydroxide/ OH - Allow the OH group is attached to a carbon atom bonded to 2 (or 3) H atoms Do not award the OH is attached to a single R/alkyl/carbon group Number Answer Additional Mark

Name butan-1-ol (2-)methylpropan-2-ol Displayed formula Nature of alcohol primary secondary (2) (2) (1) One mark for each name One mark for each correct displayed formula One mark for both alcohol types Allow CH and OH undisplayed Penalise missing Hs only once. Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • potassium dichromate((VI)) / K Cr O • sulfuric acid / H SO Note M2 depends on M1 or a near miss such as potassium permanganate or potassium dichromate with the wrong oxidation number (1) (1) Allow sodium dichromate((VI)) / Na Cr O If oxidation state is given it must be correct Do not award potassium manganate(VII) / potassium permanganate Do not award hydrochloric acid Ignore just ‘acidified’ Ignore concentration of sulfuric acid Ignore heat If no other mark is scored acidified dichromate / H + and Cr O 2− score (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • (CH ) CHCH OH + [O] (CH ) CHCHO + H O Allow C H O + [O] C H O + H O The [O] may be above the arrow Allow displayed/molecular/skeletal Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark following points: • (resulting mixture) would give a peak due to O−H bond in alcohols at 3750-3200 (cm –1 ) • would give a peak due to C=O bond in aldehydes at 1740-1720 (cm –1 ) or would give a peak due to C−H bond in CHO at 2900-2820 and/ or 2775-2700 (cm –1 ) (1) (1) Ignore any reference to the size/width of the peaks due to stretching etc Allow (resulting mixture) would give a peak due to OH (in alcohols) at 3750-3200 (cm –1 ) Do not award -OH Do not award 1720-1700 (cm –1 ) for C=O in ketones Allow any range within the range. Do not award single numbers but penalise once only If no other marks have been scored a correct wave number range for both the O-H and C=O score (1) 3750-3200 (cm –1 ) 1740-1720 (cm –1 ) (Total for Question 22 = 12 Marks) Section C Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark TiO + 2Cl + 2C → TiCl + 2CO Ignore state symbols even if incorrect Allow multiples Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • Ti is reduced and from +4 to

• hydrolysis • titanium((IV)) oxide/TiO (1) (1) Do not award hydration Ignore hydrogen chloride/HCl Allow titanium((IV))hydroxide/Ti(OH) If both name and formula/oxidation number are given they must all be correct Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

• correct repeating unit • two repeating units and extension Note M2 depends on M1 or near miss eg missing a H (1) (1) Allow adjacent pairs of methyl groups Ignore square brackets and subscript n/2 Ignore connectivity of CH Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • reagent: potassium hydroxide / KOH • conditions: aqueous/water M2 dependent on M1or near miss e.g. OH - will not score M1 but will allow access to M2. (1) (1) Allow sodium hydroxide / NaOH Ignore any reference to concentration or heat Allow dilute Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • reagent: (concentrated) phosphoric((V)) acid / H PO • reaction type: elimination/dehydration Independent marks (1) (1) Allow (concentrated) sulfuric acid Ignore heat Do not award H PO Allow passing vapour over porous pot / alumina or any surface catalyst and heat Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • reagent: phosphorus(V) chloride/ phosphorus pentachloride/PCl • equation: C H OH + PCl → C H Cl + HCl + POCl (1) (1) Allow thionyl chloride / SOCl Allow phosphorus (III) chloride / phosphorus trichloride / PCl Allow C H OH + SOCl → C H Cl + HCl + SO 3C H OH + PCl → 3C H Cl + H PO Allow skeletal, structural, displayed or molecular formulae Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • conditions: alcoholic • role of the hydroxide ions: base Note answers may be given on either line (1) (1) Allow ethanol/ethanolic Ignore heat and concentration Allow proton acceptor/reacts with H + Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • moles of carbon dioxide • conversion of cm to m • conversion of o C to K • rearrangement of the ideal gas equation • calculation of pressure and correct units given (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Examples of calculation 16 ÷ 44 = 0.36364 (mol)/ 4÷11(mol) 20 ÷ 1000000 = 2 × 10 -5 / 0.00002 (m ) 273 + 25 = 298 (K) p = nRT V 0.3636 × 8.31 × 298 = 45025000 Pa / 45025 kPa 2 × 10 -5 4.5 x 10 Pa / 45MPa Ignore SF except 1 Allow TE for answers to M1, M2 and M3 But no TE on wrong rearrangement of gas eq
